qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Linker sourcemaps can exfiltrate arbitrary local files via external-template recovery
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerable code and root cause: this finding abuses compiler-cli linker sourcemap source recovery to read local files, while the advisory fixes missing URL sanitization on translated URI attribute bindings in packages/core/src/render3/i18n/i18n_parse.ts.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's translated-attribute sanitization bypass. The session's only qualifying finding is an unrelated compiler-cli linker sourcemap file-read issue, not the missing _sanitizeUrl handling for i18n-marked URI bindings.
evidence: The advisory and Angular fix `packages/core/src/render3/i18n/i18n_parse.ts` to pass `_sanitizeUrl` for translated URI attributes and interpolations such as ``.

The sole qualifying finding traces a different code path in `packages/compiler-cli/linker/...` and `packages/compiler-cli/src/ngtsc/sourcemaps/...`, where malicious sourcemap paths cause external-template recovery to read arbitrary local files.

The session's `angular_dom_sanitization_clear/vulnerability_report.json` explicitly reviewed Angular sanitizer and translated-attribute paths and did not confirm a qualifying vulnerability in that advisory-relevant DOM/i18n family.
